root_text.sn11.14:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.sn11.14:2.1: “Bhūtapubbaṁ, bhikkhave, aññataro puriso imasmiṁyeva rājagahe manussadaliddo ahosi manussakapaṇo manussavarāko.
root_text.sn11.14:2.2: So tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye saddhaṁ samādiyi, sīlaṁ samādiyi, sutaṁ samādiyi, cāgaṁ samādiyi, paññaṁ samādiyi.
root_text.sn11.14:2.3: So tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye saddhaṁ samādiyitvā sīlaṁ samādiyitvā sutaṁ samādiyitvā cāgaṁ samādiyitvā paññaṁ samādiyitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapajji devānaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ sahabyataṁ.
root_text.sn11.14:2.4: So aññe deve atirocati vaṇṇena ceva yasasā ca.
root_text.sn11.14:2.5: Tatra sudaṁ, bhikkhave, devā tāvatiṁsā ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti:
root_text.sn11.14:2.7: Ayañhi devaputto pubbe manussabhūto samāno manussadaliddo ahosi manussakapaṇo manussavarāko;
root_text.sn11.14:2.8: so kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapanno devānaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ sahabyataṁ.
root_text.sn11.14:2.9: So aññe deve atirocati vaṇṇena ceva yasasā cā’ti.
root_text.sn11.14:3.1: Atha kho, bhikkhave, sakko devānamindo deve tāvatiṁse āmantesi:
root_text.sn11.14:3.2: ‘mā kho tumhe, mārisā, etassa devaputtassa ujjhāyittha.
root_text.sn11.14:3.3: Eso kho, mārisā, devaputto pubbe manussabhūto samāno tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye saddhaṁ samādiyi, sīlaṁ samādiyi, sutaṁ samādiyi, cāgaṁ samādiyi, paññaṁ samādiyi.
root_text.sn11.14:3.4: So tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye saddhaṁ samādiyitvā sīlaṁ samādiyitvā sutaṁ samādiyitvā cāgaṁ samādiyitvā paññaṁ samādiyitvā kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā sugatiṁ saggaṁ lokaṁ upapanno devānaṁ tāvatiṁsānaṁ sahabyataṁ.
root_text.sn11.14:3.5: So aññe deve atirocati vaṇṇena ceva yasasā cā’ti.
root_text.sn11.14:3.6: Atha kho, bhikkhave, sakko devānamindo deve tāvatiṁse anunayamāno tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imā gāthāyo abhāsi:

translation_text.sn11.14:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.sn11.14:1.2: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.sn11.14:2.1: “Once upon a time, mendicants, there was a poor person, destitute and pitiful.
translation_text.sn11.14:2.2: They took up faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.
translation_text.sn11.14:2.3: After undertaking these things, when their body broke up, after death, they were reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm, in the company of the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.sn11.14:2.4: There they outshone the other gods in beauty and glory.
translation_text.sn11.14:2.5: But the gods of the thirty-three complained, grumbled, and objected,
translation_text.sn11.14:2.7: For when this godling was a human being in their past life they were poor, destitute, and pitiful.
translation_text.sn11.14:2.8: And when their body broke up, after death, they were reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm, in the company of the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.sn11.14:2.9: Here they outshine the other gods in beauty and glory.’
translation_text.sn11.14:3.1: Then Sakka, lord of gods, addressed the gods of the thirty-three,
translation_text.sn11.14:3.2: ‘Good fellows, don’t complain about this god.
translation_text.sn11.14:3.3: When this godling was a human being in their past life they took up faith, ethics, learning, generosity, and wisdom in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.
translation_text.sn11.14:3.4: After undertaking these things, when their body broke up, after death, they’ve been reborn in a good place, a heavenly realm, in the company of the gods of the thirty-three.
translation_text.sn11.14:3.5: Here they outshine the other gods in beauty and glory.’
translation_text.sn11.14:3.6: Then Sakka, lord of gods, guiding the gods of the thirty-three, spoke this verse:
translation_text.sn11.14:4.1: ‘Whoever has faith in the Realized One,
translation_text.sn11.14:4.4: praised and loved by the noble ones;
translation_text.sn11.14:5.1: who has confidence in the Saṅgha,
translation_text.sn11.14:5.4: their life is not in vain.
translation_text.sn11.14:6.1: So let the wise devote themselves
translation_text.sn11.14:6.3: confidence, and insight into the teaching,
translation_text.sn11.14:6.4: remembering the instructions of the Buddhas.’”
